Ahaetulla.desc: Long, slender green snake with a pointed snout. Often found in shrubs.
Ahaetulla.symptoms: Mild swelling and pain. Usually not fatal to humans.

Common Krait.desc: Highly venomous nocturnal snake with white crossbands on a dark body.
Common Krait.symptoms: Neurotoxic: Abdominal pain, blindness, and respiratory failure.

Common Rat Snake.desc: Non-venomous, fast-moving snake often found near human dwellings.
Common Rat Snake.symptoms: Non-venomous. Bites may cause minor injury but no poisoning.

Indian cobra.desc: Spectacled cobra. Famous for its hood and potent neurotoxic venom.
Indian cobra.symptoms: Severe paralysis and respiratory arrest within minutes.

Indian python.desc: Large, slow-moving constrictor. Non-venomous but powerful.
Indian python.symptoms: Bite can be painful due to large teeth; no venom.

Mudu Karawala.desc: Endemic to Sri Lanka. Highly venomous and active at night.

Russell's viper.desc: Responsible for most fatal bites in Sri Lanka. Very aggressive.
Russell's viper.symptoms: Haemotoxic: Internal bleeding, kidney failure, and tissue death.
